DISCOVERY 612, DEFENDANTS DAVID MEDLIN, DANIEL STEA'S MOTION TO COMPEL THE APPEARANCE OF PLAINTIFF ANGEL MANOSCA IS GRANTED. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $675, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. DEFENDANTS' OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATION OF ERIC HARTMENT ARE OVERRULED. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR DETAILS)

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT DAVID MEDLIN INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA MEDLING & STEA'S DEMURRER AND SPECIAL DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1. TO PLEAD FRAUD WITH GREATER SPECIFICITY AS TO ALLEGED ORAL MISREPRESENTATION, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE WHAT THEY WERE, WHO MADE THEM, AND WHEN. AS TO ALLEGED OVER BILLINGS, PLAINTIFF MUST PROVIDE AT LEAST AN EXAMPLE OF AN ALLEGEDLY FALSE CHARGE. SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 2 AND 4. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT DAVID MEDLIN'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITH ONE FINAL OPPORTUNITY TO AMEND IF PLAINTIFF IS ALLEGING IN GOOD FAITH THAT EACH DEFENDANT ORALLY MADE EACH ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATION EACH MONTH, HE MUST EXPRESSLY PLEAD OR OTHERWISE SO PLEAD MISREPRESENTATION WITH THE SPECIFICITY PREVIOUSLY ORDERED. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

Mo To Compel The Appearance Of Pltf Angel Manosca
Off Calendar Dec-12-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Defendants David Medlin, Daniel Stea's Motion To Compel The Appearance Of Plaintiff Angel Manosca is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to appear for his deposition on January 7, 2009, until completed, including up to January 9, 2009. The court finds that Plaintiff's failure to appear and to make arrangements for his appearance in a timely fashion was a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $675, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Defendants' objections to the declaration of Eric Hartment are overruled. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-01-2008 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. ERIC F. HARTMAN, TEL. NO. (408) 297-7254 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JOSH ROSENTHAL OF THE LAW FIRM OF MEDLIN & HARGRAVE, TEL. NO. (510) 832-2900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS AND MOVING PARTIES.

RULING- A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS DANIEL STEA INDIVIDUALLY AND D B A MEDLIN & STEA AND DAVID MEDLIN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHIFTED THE BURDEN. THE FACT THAT THE FEES WERE LESS THAN THE ULTIMATE RECOVERY IS NOT ENOUGH TO SHOW THAT THERE WAS NO DAMAGE FROM THE ALLEGED FRAUD. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189 =301/PJB/BD

MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANT DAVID MEDLIN INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA MEDLING & STEA'S DEMURRER AND SPECIAL DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1.
